common.name: Name
common.no_permissions: You do not have permission to access this page.
common.module_unavailable_desc: This module is not yet available in this version of the platform.

users.title: Participants
users.subtitle: Manage platform participants
users.intro: Participants are the accounts that access the platform. Each account has a role that defines what they can do: a player receives injects, a facilitator runs the exercise, an observer follows along without taking action. You can also assign them to a team and add tags to make inject targeting easier.
users.passwordEdit: New password (leave blank to keep current)
users.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this participant?

teams.title: Teams
teams.subtitle: Manage participating teams
teams.intro: Teams let you group participants and send injects to them collectively. A team can represent a department, a directorate, or a functional group. Each participant can be assigned to a team, and injects can be targeted at an entire team at once.
teams.no_teams_found: No teams found for this filter
teams.all_members_added: All participants are already in the team
teams.confirm_delete_orphan: Delete {{count}} team(s) without exercise? This action is irreversible.

audit.title: Audit & Compliance
audit.subtitle: Audit log and participant action tracking

intros.general: These settings define the default values suggested when creating an exercise. You can update them at any time without affecting exercises that have already been created.
intros.inject_types: Inject types represent the communication channels simulated during an exercise: email, SMS, phone call, social media… For each channel, you choose the accepted file formats and the simulator that handles it.
intros.sources: Select the news sources that will feed your exercise timelines. You can filter by country and category (press, TV, government) to match the context of your scenarios.
intros.timelines: Configure the phases suggested by default when creating an exercise. A phase represents a key period in the scenario — for example: detection, response, crisis communication. These settings do not affect exercises that have already been created.
intros.simulators: Simulators replicate everyday communication tools during the exercise: email client, social media, TV channel… Enable only the ones you need and customise their appearance for each scenario.
intros.security: Manage platform access rules here: session duration, maximum login attempts, and minimum password length. API keys allow external tools to connect to the platform securely, without requiring a user account.
intros.email: Connect an email server (SMTP) so the platform can notify participants. If no server is configured, email notifications will not be sent.

exercises.title: Exercises
exercises.subtitle: Manage your crisis exercises
exercises.subtitleParticipant: Join a crisis exercise to start your mission
exercises.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete {{name}} and all associated data.
exercises.deleteError: Cannot delete exercise. Please try again.
exercises.participantInfo: Click "Join" to access the exercise interface and start your mission. You'll have access to the timeline, messaging, team chat and other tools.

intros.new: Define the main characteristics of your exercise here. You can refine all the details — scenario, participants, injects — from the configuration page after creation.
intros.step_socle: Choose the type of incident to simulate, the exercise duration, the expected maturity level, and the run mode. These base settings guide the rest of the configuration.
intros.step_scenario: Write the story of your exercise: the situation participants walk into, the learning objective you are pursuing, and the broad escalation axes (technical, communication, legal…).
intros.step_actors: Assign participants to this exercise and define their roles. You can initialise the full crisis cell in one click, then fine-tune individual roles.
intros.step_timeline: Structure the exercise by phases, then create the injects — events and messages sent to participants at precise moments in the simulation.
intros.step_simulators: Choose which communication channels are active during the exercise. Each simulator (messaging, social media, TV…) can be toggled on or off to fit your scenario.
intros.step_validation: Check that all sections are properly filled in before launching the exercise. Items flagged as incomplete must be resolved to ensure smooth execution.
intros.contacts: The crisis directory lists useful contacts for the exercise: external stakeholders, service providers, authorities… Fill it in advance so participants can look them up when needed.
intros.media: The media library centralises files used in your injects: images, videos, audio, documents. Upload your assets here to reuse them easily across different injects.
intros.inject_bank: The inject bank is a library of reusable content shared across exercises. Create generic injects here, then import them into an exercise and adapt them to your scenario.

observer.readOnly: Read-only access to exercises. Select an exercise to observe it.

profile.title: My profile
profile.display_name_hint: Visible in the sidebar and messages. Leave blank to use the login.
profile.avatar_url_hint: Public link to an image (JPG, PNG, WebP…).

organisation.title: Organisation
organisation.intro_identity: Describe your organisation: its name, sector and general context. This information is used to personalise scenarios and documents generated during exercises.
organisation.intro_it_context: Fill in the technical details of your infrastructure: network domain, IP address ranges, key servers… These details make scenarios more realistic and relevant to your actual environment.
organisation.intro_bia: The BIA (Business Impact Analysis) lists your critical processes and how long they can tolerate an interruption. It helps focus scenarios on what matters most to your organisation.
organisation.tenant_scoped_info: The values displayed and saved here are now tenant-scoped for <bold>{{tenant}}</bold>.
organisation.name: Organisation name
organisation.logo_url_hint: Direct URL to an image file (PNG, SVG, JPG). Do not use a webpage URL.
organisation.description_placeholder: Briefly describe the business, context and stakes of the organisation...
organisation.keywords_placeholder: sector, technologies, domains, subsidiaries, IS, IOC...
organisation.tech_stack_hint: Describe the critical technologies of the organisation. This information helps the AI generate realistic injects.
organisation.it_context_hint: This information allows the generation engine to produce credible elements: logs, emails, SOC alerts, technical incidents with real server names, IP addresses and domains.

tips.windows_domain: Internal domain name of your Windows / Active Directory network. This is the suffix used to identify machines on the local network (e.g. corp.acme.local). Your IT manager can provide this.
tips.public_domain: Domain name visible on the Internet for your website and emails (e.g. acme.com). This is the address your clients and partners use.
tips.mail_domain: The part after @ in your professional email addresses (e.g. for firstname@acme.com, enter acme.com). May differ from the public domain if you use a dedicated subdomain.
tips.internal_ip_ranges: IP address ranges of your internal network, in CIDR notation (e.g. 192.168.1.0/24 covers addresses from .0 to .255). An IP address identifies each device on the network, like a phone number.
tips.dmz_ip_ranges: An intermediate network zone between the Internet and your internal network, hosting servers accessible from outside (website, email…). Enter the IP ranges of this zone in CIDR notation.
tips.domain_controllers: Servers that manage user accounts and access rights on your Windows network. Enter their full names, one per line (e.g. DC01.corp.acme.local).
tips.server_naming: Your server naming convention, with brief explanations. Helps understand their role at a glance (e.g. SRV-FILE-01 = file server #1).
tips.tech_dependencies: Tools and software your infrastructure relies on: antivirus, backup solution, firewall, SIEM… List the products used and their role.
tips.cloud_providers: Cloud platforms in use (AWS, Azure, OVHcloud…), the hosted services and geographic region (e.g. Azure France Central for Microsoft 365 email).
tips.critical_apps: Software essential to your organisation's operations: ERP, CRM, email, intranet… If they go down, the impact is felt immediately.

organisation.bia_hint: Define the critical processes of your organisation. These BCP/DRP indicators (MTPD, RTO, RPO) help contextualise TTX exercises and generate realistic scenarios.

bia.no_processes: No processes defined. Click "Add a process" to get started.
